problem.title: Food Waste Across the Supply Chain
problem.description: An estimated third of food produced in the U.S. is lost or wasted between farm and fork, even as tens of millions of households face food insecurity — a mismatch of logistics and policy, not supply.

solution.title: National Food Loss & Waste Strategy
solution.description: Youth chapters are pushing city-level food-waste toolkits and supply-chain recovery programs aligned with the EPA's national strategy, redirecting surplus food to communities that need it.

problem.title: Deforestation Pressure on Family Farms
problem.description: Rural youth in the Amazon and Caatinga face economic pressure to migrate to cities as mechanized industrial agriculture expands, squeezing out smallholder and agroforestry livelihoods.

solution.title: Agroforestry Restoration Corridors
solution.description: Youth-backed agroforestry projects are combining native and agricultural species on restored land — tens of thousands of seedlings planted so far — giving young farmers income without clearing standing forest.

problem.title: Smallholder Vulnerability to Climate Shocks
problem.description: Smallholder farmers produce over 80% of Nigeria's food on more than 70 million hectares, yet over 33 million Nigerians are projected to face food insecurity, exposing how fragile that system is.

solution.title: Youth-Led Agroecology Capacity Building
solution.description: State-level programs are training young people from agricultural organizations in agroecology practices, building local capacity to stabilize yields against climate volatility.

problem.title: Groundwater Depletion from Irrigation
problem.description: Agriculture consumes 80-90% of India's water while surface irrigation efficiency sits near 38%, and India draws nearly a quarter of the world's groundwater — a system running faster than it can recharge.

solution.title: Drip Irrigation & 'Catch the Rain' Expansion
solution.description: The Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana is scaling drip and sprinkler irrigation, paired with the Jal Shakti Abhiyan rainwater-catchment push now active across 148 districts.

problem.title: Drought Cycles Threatening Pastoralist Livelihoods
problem.description: Increasingly frequent drought is straining pastoralist and smallholder communities across Kenya's drylands, where rainfall variability now directly drives food insecurity.

solution.title: School Farms & Climate-Smart Agriculture Strategy
solution.description: Kenya's Climate Smart Agriculture Strategy and youth-run school farm programs are building resilience to temperature and rainfall shifts while giving young people hands-on food-production skills.

problem.title: Youth Leaving Agriculture
problem.description: As Bhutan pursues its 100%-organic ambitions, youth unemployment and the drift away from farming threaten the labor base needed to sustain organic transitions in rural gewogs.

solution.title: Seeds of Innovation Fellowship
solution.description: Launched in August 2024 with FAO and the Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock, the WFF Bhutan Youth Chapter's fellowship backs grassroots youth-led solutions, alongside Nu 10 million in annual startup support.

problem.title: Aging Farmer Population, Falling Self-Sufficiency
problem.description: Japan's farming population is among the world's oldest, and food self-sufficiency has been declining for decades, leaving the country increasingly reliant on imports for staple caloric needs.

solution.title: Smart Farms & Food-Waste Reduction Tech
solution.description: Youth-backed precision-agriculture pilots and IoT-driven smart farms aim to make small aging farms dramatically more productive per worker, while national food-waste laws push retailers toward near-zero surplus.

problem.title: Opaque Supply Chains, Circularity Gaps
problem.description: Consumers and regulators increasingly can't trace how food moves from farm to shelf, making it hard to hold supply chains accountable for waste, emissions, or labor conditions.

solution.title: Circular Food Systems Pilots
solution.description: Chapter-backed circular-economy pilots are mapping supply chains end-to-end and diverting food byproducts back into local production loops instead of landfill.

problem.title: Erosion of Agricultural Biodiversity
problem.description: Decades of intensive monoculture have narrowed the varieties of crops grown commercially, putting culinary heritage and genetic resilience against pests and drought both at risk.

solution.title: Heritage Crop & Short Supply Chain Advocacy
solution.description: Youth-led advocacy is reviving heritage crop varieties and building short farm-to-table supply chains that reward biodiversity instead of penalizing it.

problem.title: Post-Brexit Policy Gaps & Youth Disengagement
problem.description: Shifting agricultural subsidy schemes since Brexit have left many young growers uncertain of long-term support, while urban populations remain disconnected from food production entirely.

solution.title: Urban Farming & Youth Policy Engagement
solution.description: Chapter members are running urban farming pilots in city plots while pushing for youth seats at the table in national food-policy consultations.

problem.title: Youth Unemployment Meets Food Insecurity
problem.description: South Africa's high youth unemployment and food insecurity are deeply linked — the National Youth Policy 2030 and Agriculture Master Plan both name youth exclusion from agrifood systems as a core barrier.

solution.title: Youth Micro-Farming Entrepreneurship Pilot
solution.description: The WFF South Africa Youth Chapter, launched in Johannesburg in November 2025, is backing climate-smart micro-farming pilots that train unemployed youth as agritech entrepreneurs.

problem.title: Public Perception Gap in Agriculture
problem.description: Egypt's agrifood transformation is held back less by resources than by perception — agriculture is often seen by young people as a fallback, not a viable, innovative career path.

solution.title: Egypt's Third Agricultural Revolution
solution.description: The WFF Egypt Youth Chapter has reached over 1,000 students and trained teachers to reframe agriculture as innovation-driven, while incubating youth-led food startups toward what it calls Egypt's Third Agricultural Revolution.

problem.title: Youth Excluded from Food Policy Decisions
problem.description: Climate change is reshaping Australian agriculture faster than policy can adapt, and the chapter identifies limited youth voice in food-system decision-making as a compounding risk.

solution.title: Youth Food Charter & Field Notes
solution.description: The chapter's Youth Food Charter captures grassroots voices nationwide, paired with the Field Notes storytelling project and on-farm learning experiences that put youth directly into policy conversations.

problem.title: Rural-Urban Migration Draining Farm Labor
problem.description: Decades of rural-to-urban migration have thinned the young workforce in China's farming regions, leaving an aging population to sustain national food production.

solution.title: Rural Revitalization & Agri-Tech Scaling
solution.description: National rural revitalization policy is pairing with youth-led agri-tech pilots — automation, e-commerce direct-to-consumer sales — to make farming economically attractive again for young people.

problem.title: Loss of Traditional Polyculture Systems
problem.description: Traditional milpa systems — maize, beans, and squash grown together — are giving way to monoculture, reducing both dietary diversity and the soil resilience these intercropping systems provided.

solution.title: Milpa Revival & Smallholder Cooperatives
solution.description: Youth cooperatives are reviving milpa polyculture on smallholder plots, pairing traditional knowledge with modern market access to make biodiverse farming commercially viable again.

problem.title: Urbanization Outpacing Food System Capacity
problem.description: Changing consumption patterns and rising food demand from Indonesia's 270+ million residents, driven by population growth and urbanization, are outpacing the current agrifood system's capacity.

solution.title: Indonesia Food Academy & Youth Innovation Challenge
solution.description: The WFF Indonesia Youth Chapter's six-month Food Academy and Youth Innovation Challenge work across six divisions to regenerate young farmers and align with the national Food System Transformation pathway.

problem.title: Food Insecurity in Northern & Indigenous Communities
problem.description: Remote northern and Indigenous communities face some of the country's highest food costs and lowest fresh-food access, a gap conventional supply chains haven't closed.

solution.title: Indigenous-Led Food Sovereignty Partnerships
solution.description: Chapter members are partnering directly with Indigenous-led food sovereignty programs, combining traditional harvesting knowledge with cold-chain logistics investment in the north.

problem.title: Monoculture Pressure on the Pampas
problem.description: Export-driven soy monoculture across the Pampas has squeezed out diversified smallholder farming, concentrating both land use and climate risk.

solution.title: Youth Agroecology Cooperatives
solution.description: Youth cooperatives are piloting diversified, agroecological rotations on smaller plots, aiming to prove that biodiversity and profitability aren't a trade-off.

problem.title: Food Waste, Climate Change & Unequal Access
problem.description: The chapter names food waste, climate change, gender gaps, and unequal access to food as Italy's core interlocking agrifood challenges.

solution.title: Nine Thematic Working Groups
solution.description: 294 members and over 900 trained young people work across nine groups spanning sustainability, education, policy, innovation, and gender equality, engaging bodies like the UN Committee on World Food Security.

problem.title: Climate Extremes Threaten Rice Heritage Systems
problem.description: Increasingly erratic monsoon patterns are straining Thailand's heritage rice-growing systems, with smallholders bearing the brunt of yield volatility.

solution.title: Agri-Tech Climate Resilience Initiative
solution.description: New agri-tech partnerships are pairing climate-finance investment with smallholder digitalization tools to help rice farmers adapt planting cycles to shifting weather.

problem.title: Terraced Farmland Vulnerable to Erratic Rainfall
problem.description: Rwanda's steep, terraced farmland is highly exposed to erratic rainfall and erosion, putting smallholder yields at risk as climate patterns shift.

solution.title: Climate-Smart Agriculture Investment Plan
solution.description: Launched in June 2025 with the Rwanda Green Fund and IFC, the CSA Investment Plan targets over 83,000 hectares for climate resilience, reaching an estimated 170,000+ farmers through efficient water use and improved soil health.

problem.title: Shrinking Arable Land, Aging Rural Population
problem.description: Rapid urbanization has shrunk South Korea's arable land while its rural farming population continues to age, squeezing traditional production capacity.

solution.title: Vertical Farms & Smart Agriculture
solution.description: Youth-backed vertical farming and smart-farm technology are pushing yield-per-square-meter far beyond traditional methods, making urban-adjacent food production viable again.

problem.title: Typhoon Exposure Across Coastal Food Systems
problem.description: Coastal fisheries and farmland face recurring typhoon damage, making climate resilience — not just productivity — the central food-security challenge.

solution.title: Young Farmers Challenge Program
solution.description: The Department of Agriculture's Young Farmers Challenge Program provides financial grants to young Filipinos building typhoon-resilient farming and fisheries livelihoods.

problem.title: Scaling Alt-Protein Beyond Pilot Stage
problem.description: Sweden's alt-protein and zero-waste food research is strong at the pilot stage but still struggles to scale into mainstream affordable supply chains.

solution.title: Community-Supported Agroecology Living Lab
solution.description: A CSA model at Alnarp's Agroecology Farm now supports 60+ households as a living lab, offering internships and workshops that feed alt-protein and zero-waste research into practice.

problem.title: Disconnected Coastal, Highland & Amazon Food Systems
problem.description: Peru's coastal, highland, and Amazon communities face distinct climate and market pressures, and the chapter names weak regional collaboration as a barrier to shared solutions.

solution.title: Youth Food Lab
solution.description: The WFF Peru Youth Chapter's Youth Food Lab connects young people across all three regions, scaling youth-driven innovation and entrepreneurship through shared institutional partnerships.

problem.title: Rainfall Risk for Young Sahel Farmers
problem.description: Sahel rainfall volatility exposes young farmers to yield and income shocks with little financial safety net, discouraging youth from staying in agriculture.

solution.title: Agri-Jeunes Youth Insurance & Training
solution.description: The CNAAS Agri-Jeunes initiative trained and insured over 2,000 young farmers in 2024 against rainfall deficits and yield loss, building a financial floor under youth-led farming.

problem.title: Saltwater Intrusion in the Mekong Delta
problem.description: Rising sea levels are pushing saltwater further into the Mekong Delta's rice paddies, threatening one of Southeast Asia's most productive rice-growing regions.

solution.title: Climate-Resilient Rice Innovation
solution.description: Youth-backed trials of salt-tolerant rice varieties and adaptive planting calendars are helping delta farmers keep pace with encroaching salinity.

problem.title: Export Sustainability Under Scrutiny
problem.description: As a major food exporter, New Zealand faces growing international scrutiny over the environmental footprint of pastoral farming, pressuring the sector to prove sustainability credentials.

solution.title: Regenerative Pastoral & Indigenous Partnerships
solution.description: Youth-led regenerative grazing pilots, developed alongside Māori food-system knowledge, aim to cut environmental impact while keeping export-grade productivity.
